title: Order 2024/3 of 8 August 2024
text:
List of Cases No. 33 Order 2024/3
INTERNATIONAL TRIBUNAL FOR THE LAW OF THE SEA
YEAR 2024
8 August 2024
THE "ZHENG HE" CASE
(LUXEMBOURG v. MEXICO)
ORDER
The President of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea,
Having regard to articles 24 and 27 of the Statute of the Tribunal,
Having regard to articles 44, 45, 46, 59 and 60 of the Rules of the Tribunal,
Makes the following Order.
Whereas, by an Application dated 3 June 2024 and filed with the Registry of
the Tribunal on 4 June 2024, Luxembourg instituted proceedings against Mexico in a
dispute regarding the vessel "Zheng He";
Whereas Luxembourg has appointed Ms Annabel Rossi as Agent and
Ms Elisabeth Relave-Svendsen as Co-Agent; and whereas Mexico has appointed
Mr Alejandro Celorio Alcantara as Agent and Mr Francisco Jose Quiroga Fernandez,
Mr Miguel Angel Reyes Moncayo and Mr Alfonso Ascencio Herrera as Co-Agents;

2
Whereas video consultations were held by the President of the Tribunal with
the representatives of the Parties on 6 August 2024 to ascertain the views of the
Parties with regard to questions of procedure in respect of the case;
Whereas, during these consultations, the Parties agreed that the written
proceedings shall consist of a Memorial presented by Luxembourg and a Counter
Memorial presented by Mexico and that the filing of further written pleadings should
be decided at a later stage;
Whereas the Parties further agreed that 10 February 2025 should be the time
limit for the filing of the Memorial by Luxembourg and 11 August 2025 should be the
time limit for the filing of the Counter-Memorial by Mexico;
THE PRESIDENT
Taking into account the agreement of the Parties,
Fixes the following time limits for the filing of the Memorial and the Counter
Memorial:
10 February 2025 for the Memorial of Luxembourg;
11 August 2025 for the Counter-Memorial of Mexico;
and
Reserves the subsequent procedure for further decision.
Done in English and French, both texts being authoritative, in the Free and
Hanseatic City of Hamburg, this eighth day of August, two thousand and twenty-four,

3
in three copies, one of which will be placed in the archives of the Tribunal and the
others transmitted to the Government of Luxembourg and the Government of Mexico,
respectively.
Tomas HEIDAR
President
1i~CHS'
Registrar
court: International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)
